summary:
The parotid duct travels above the masseter muscle before puncturing through the buccinator muscle en route to exiting close to the maxillary second molar region within the mouth.
To clarify based directly from medical literature referenced earlier, after traversing superiorly relative position alongside/overlying masseter structure, actual penetration occurs via direct contact resulting in passage thoroughness involving muscular tissue specifically identified here being referred-to correctly using more accurate language indicating not only positional relationship but active engagement leading up final point where salivary secretion initiates external communication opening pathway allowing saliva release facilitating digestive process enhancing overall function
